items.blurb: A hand-carved eraser stamp of a tiny baby drifting on the moon — artist-made in Japan and quietly magical on any journal page.
Wanderlust in stamp form: a hand-carved moon-series design that turns envelopes and planner pages into little travel diaries.
Tokyo's skinny three-storey houses, shrunk into blush-pink petit stickers your planner will happily move into.
Tiny photo-style stickers in soft pinks — instant scrapbook charm for journal spreads that need a little nostalgia.
Scottish watercolour florals meet Japanese washi in this mt x bluebellgray roll — a painterly stripe for journals and gift wrap.
Lisa Larson's shaggy lion prowls along this mt washi tape — Scandinavian charm by the metre for planners and parcels.
mt's torehari tape swaps washi for translucent tracing paper — a soft mosaic pattern that layers like stained glass on the page.
Translucent maple leaves on tracing-paper tape — layer it over journal pages for an instant breezy-summer glow.
Soft overlapping circles on see-through tracing-paper tape — the minimalist's way to add texture without the noise.
Delicate lines on translucent tape that layer beautifully — a quiet workhorse for clean, airy journal spreads.
Little swimmers glide across this translucent tracing-paper tape — a splash of summery whimsy for your spreads.
Folk-inspired dots on airy tracing-paper tape — a tiny alpine border that plays nicely with every planner palette.
Geometric box patterns on translucent tape — layer, overlap and watch your journal pages get quietly architectural.
A zipper motif running down see-through tracing-paper tape — the playful border your planner didn't know it needed.
From an illustrated series about journeys toward hope, this small square stamp presses a whole gentle story onto the page.
A dreamy little sheep stamp from the journey-to-hopes series — hand-drawn softness for letters, tags and journals.
MIDORI's rub-on transfers lay illustrated story vignettes flat on the page — no sticker edges, all storybook charm.
Rub, press, and dainty desserts appear seamlessly on your planner page — MIDORI transfer stickers at their most delicious.
Pressure-transfer florals that sit flush on the paper — a MIDORI trick for journal pages that look hand-illustrated.
Fifteen squares of softly textured paper for collage, wrapping tiny gifts, or giving journal spreads that layered-atelier look.
A sunny wreath-and-botanical sticker sheet in warm yellows — limited-run foliage for planner corners everywhere.
Cool-toned botanical wreath stickers, printed in Japan and gone when they're gone — greenery for your tidiest spreads.
A cheerful spread of illustrated meals in sticker form — mark dinner plans and cafe dates with maximum appetite.
San-X's shy corner-dwellers peek out of every die-cut edge of this washi tape — softly chaotic cuteness by the roll.
Die-cut washi starring the Sumikko Gurashi crew — because even tape deserves a quiet life in the corner of your planner.
Bright illustrated blooms on a spring-limited sticker sheet — instant garden party for weekly spreads.
Cakes, parfaits and pastel treats in planner-sticker form — dessert cravings you can commit to paper guilt-free.
Nishi Shuku's playful art on crystal-clear PET tape — it floats over any page colour like illustrated confetti.
A tiny folded card carried by an illustrated bird — the sweetest possible vehicle for a thank-you or hello.
Letterpress-printed octagon label cards with vintage-apothecary charm — for collage, gift tags and very short love notes.
Watercolour lipsticks, polishes and potions on one limited sticker sheet — vanity-table glamour for your planner.
Painterly croissants and crusty loaves in sticker form — a limited-run bakery haul with zero crumbs.
Loose watercolour blooms on a limited sticker sheet — pressed-flower romance without the flower press.
Rieko Oka's gentle rainy-day illustration on a petite folding card — for notes that deserve a soft landing.
A miniature illustrated garden folded into a message card — thank-yous and invitations, freshly planted.
Two Lin Chia Ning stamps in one limited set — a pattern base plus a stationery motif to layer into tiny inked scenes.
A limited two-stamp duo with vintage postage vibes — background pattern plus motif, built for layered mail art.
Coffee-themed rubber stamps by Lin Chia Ning, sold as a limited pair — brew a little cafe corner onto every page.
Six crisp Lin Chia Ning stamps riffing on vintage postage — a serious little toolkit for collage and mail-art devotees.
Naoko Nakanishi's giraffe and leopard share billing on these petite Classiky note cards — safari-chic in miniature.
The Sumikko gang runs a diner on this glossy sticker sheet — shy characters, shiny finish, maximum planner joy.
Shiny diner-themed stickers starring San-X's corner-loving crew — comfort food and comfort characters in one sheet.
mizutama's hand-drawn cookies parade down this washi roll — sweet enough to make your planner smell like a bakery.
A limited run of mizutama's cookie washi — cheerful biscuit illustrations by the metre while stock lasts.
Two A5 sheets of mizutama strawberry cakes — one pre-cut, one giant sticker to snip however your heart desires.
mizutama's cookie deco stickers, two sheets deep — including a big cut-your-own sheet for freeform biscuit arranging.
A two-sheet mizutama cake buffet in A5 — pre-cut slices plus one big sticker canvas for creative cutting.
Vintage-encyclopedia reindeer march down this Christmas-limited mt washi — scholarly, seasonal, and quietly festive.
Rub these on and silver-foil poinsettias bloom straight onto the page — winter-limited shimmer with no sticker edges.
Gold-foil robins transfer onto paper with a rub of the finger — a winter-limited gleam for cards and journals.
Holiday motifs in rub-on gold foil — festive gilding for envelopes and journal pages, gone after the season.
Winter-limited rub-on transfers with a gift-wrapped theme — seamless illustrated details for the season's letters.
Poinsettias that press flat onto the page like printed art — MU's winter-limited rub-on stickers do the work.
Two big sheets of New Year cheer — oversized festive stickers for decking out January spreads in style.
Twenty washi-paper flakes of lucky Japanese New Year icons — tiny fortunes to scatter across cards and journals.
Cookie-print mino washi letter paper with matching envelopes — thirty tiny sheets for notes as sweet as they look.
Kurogoma's charming flower illustrations circle this washi roll — a small burst of artist-drawn joy for any project.
mt's Flutter tape in a slim, breezy pattern — subtle movement for the edges of your tidiest spreads.
Dots that softly fade along mt's slim deco tape — a gradient border for the pastel-minimal planner set.
A gara-gara memo pad from Furukawa's Retro Diary line — nostalgic old-Japan patterns on every tear-off sheet.
Retro Diary charm in memo-pad form — vintage Japanese patterns that make even grocery lists feel nostalgic.
Furukawa's nostalgia-soaked gara-gara memo pad — old-days Japan, one tear-away note at a time.
A Retro Diary memo pad with lovingly old-fashioned patterns — scraps of nostalgia for planner collages and quick notes.
Limited-edition Retro Diary memo sheets — vintage Japanese flavour for journaling, collage, or very stylish to-dos.
A red Retro Diary memo pad that treats your task list to vintage Japanese styling — productivity, but make it nostalgic.
Blue retro to-do sheets from Furukawa's Retro Diary series — crossing things off has never looked so vintage.
The girly edition of the Retro Diary to-do pad — sweet vintage flourishes around every checkbox.
A sunny yellow to-do pad in Furukawa's nostalgic Retro Diary style — cheerful structure for scattered days.
A die-cut mizutama paper clip with cafe-time charm — part bookmark, part tiny barista for your pages.
mizutama's die-cut dog clip holds your place with a wagging tail — limited stock, unlimited charm.
A musical die-cut paper clip by mizutama — clip it on and your reading list gets its own tiny soundtrack.
Twenty write-on memo clips in three mizutama designs — bookmark, tab and sticky-note energy all in one clever set.
Maruco Art's watercolour cafe scenes as rub-on transfers — lattes and pastries pressed seamlessly onto the page.
Cats among watercolour flowers, transferred straight onto paper — Maruco Art's rub-on stickers for feline romantics.
A watercolour trip through Japan in rub-on sticker form — torii gates and travel dreams for your journal pages.
Thirty-two generously sized bread stickers — croissants and loaves big enough to be the main character of any spread.
A traveling cat takes an autumn road trip across this illustrated postcard — seasonal Japan, feline chauffeur included.
A cozy autumn afternoon, as enjoyed by a traveling cat — Toshinori Mori's postcard series at its most snuggly.
A polar bear on postal duty, carved into a rubber stamp by momoro — first-class cuteness for every envelope.
momoro's fox clutches a love letter on this small square stamp — the Send More Mail series at its most smitten.
